headline: New Deere CFO Appointment Puts Focus On Valuation And Capital Allocation
summary: Deere & Company (NYSE:DE) has appointed Brent Norwood as its new Chief Financial Officer. The appointment follows a comprehensive internal and external search. Norwood has been involved with major acquisitions and Deere's Smart Industrial strategy. Deere is a major player in agricultural, construction, and forestry equipment, as well as precision agriculture technology. The CFO change arrives as equipment makers continue to focus on productivity, automation, and digital tools that can help...

headline: Forestry Equipment Industry Report 2026: Market to Surpass $14.5 Billion by 2030, Driven by Strategic Initiatives, New Product Launches, and Expansion Into Emerging Markets
summary: The global forestry equipment market, valued at $11.33 billion in 2024, is projected to reach $14.51 billion by 2030, growing at a CAGR of around 4%. This growth is driven by increasing demand for sustainable practices, bioenergy expansion, and innovations like electric and hybrid equipment. Segmentation by product highlights felling equipment's leading market share, while oil-powered machinery dominates by power source. headline: Deere Keeps Paying Out, but Is That Dividend Safe?
summary: Deere (NYSE: DE), widely considered the world’s largest maker of agricultural equipment, pays its next quarterly dividend of $1.62 per share on May 8, 2026. With the payout held flat at $1.62 across the past several quarters and the ag cycle bottoming, income-focused investors want to know if the dividend is secure. Let’s find out. Dividend ... Deere Keeps Paying Out, but Is That Dividend Safe?

headline: Ascent Global Logistics Earns Recognition as a John Deere "Partner-Level Supplier" for the 11th Consecutive Year
summary: Ascent Global Logistics ("Ascent") has earned recognition as a Partner-level supplier for 2025 in the John Deere Achieving Excellence Program, marking the 11th consecutive year the company has received this top supplier rating. The Partner-level status is Deere & Company's highest supplier rating. The Belleville, MI-based company was selected for the honor in recognition of its dedication to providing products and service of outstanding quality as well as its commitment to continuous improvement

thumbnail.caption: Daryl Knight, Chief Commercial Officer, and Greg Netter, SVP of Strategic Accounts, accept the Partner-level Supplier award on behalf of Ascent Global Logistics during the John Deere Achieving Excellence Program ceremony at Deere & Company World Headquarters on April 16, 2026.

headline: Deere (DE) Dips More Than Broader Market: What You Should Know
summary: In the closing of the recent trading day, Deere (DE) stood at $580.54, denoting a -1.88% move from the preceding trading day.
